title: Liraglutide and cardiovascular outcomes in type 2 diabetes
abstract: BACKGROUND: The long-term cardiovascular effects of liraglutide in type 2 diabetes are unknown. METHODS: We randomly assigned 9,340 patients with T2D and high cardiovascular risk to liraglutide or placebo over a median of 3.8 years. RESULTS: The primary composite outcome occurred in fewer patients in the liraglutide group (HR 0.87, 95% CI 0.78-0.97, p=0.01). Cardiovascular death was reduced (HR 0.78). CONCLUSIONS: Liraglutide reduces cardiovascular events.

title: Oral semaglutide and cardiovascular outcomes in patients with type 2 diabetes
abstract: BACKGROUND: The cardiovascular safety of oral semaglutide is unknown. METHODS: We assigned 3,183 patients with T2D to oral semaglutide or placebo over a median of 15.9 months. RESULTS: Oral semaglutide was non-inferior to placebo for MACE (HR 0.79, 95% CI 0.57-1.11). CONCLUSIONS: Oral semaglutide was non-inferior regarding cardiovascular safety.

title: Cardiovascular, mortality, and kidney outcomes with GLP-1 receptor agonists in patients with type 2 diabetes: a systematic review and meta-analysis of randomised clinical trials
abstract: BACKGROUND: GLP-1 RAs have shown cardiovascular benefits in individual trials, but a comprehensive meta-analysis is needed. METHODS: Meta-analysis of 8 cardiovascular outcome trials with 60,080 participants. RESULTS: GLP-1 RAs reduced MACE by 14% (HR 0.86, 95% CI 0.79-0.94), all-cause mortality by 12% (HR 0.88, 95% CI 0.82-0.94), and kidney composite outcomes by 21% (HR 0.79, 95% CI 0.73-0.87). CONCLUSIONS: GLP-1 RAs reduce cardiovascular, mortality, and kidney outcomes.

title: Effect of liraglutide, a glucagon-like peptide-1 analogue, on left ventricular function in stable chronic heart failure patients with and without diabetes (LIVE) - a multicentre, double-blind, randomised, placebo-controlled trial
abstract: AIMS: To evaluate the effect of liraglutide on cardiac function in heart failure. METHODS: 241 patients with HFrEF randomized to liraglutide or placebo for 24 weeks. RESULTS: No improvement in LVEF (mean difference 0.1%, 95% CI -2.1 to 2.3, p=0.94). More serious adverse events in liraglutide arm (17% vs 11%). CONCLUSIONS: Liraglutide does not improve LVEF in HFrEF.

title: Effects of liraglutide on clinical stability among patients with advanced heart failure and reduced ejection fraction: a randomized clinical trial
abstract: IMPORTANCE: GLP-1 agonists may benefit heart failure patients. METHODS: RCT of 300 patients with advanced HFrEF, followed for 180 days. RESULTS: No difference in clinical stability (primary rank score 1.30 vs 1.31, p=0.96). No benefit on 6MWD or quality of life. CONCLUSIONS: Liraglutide did not improve clinical stability in advanced HFrEF.

title: Liraglutide and renal outcomes in type 2 diabetes
abstract: BACKGROUND: GLP-1 RAs may have renal benefits. METHODS: Post hoc analysis of the LEADER trial (n=9,340). RESULTS: Liraglutide reduced new-onset macroalbuminuria by 26% (HR 0.74, 95% CI 0.60-0.91, p=0.004). eGFR decline was slower (-2.4 vs -3.3 mL/min/1.73m2/year). CONCLUSIONS: Liraglutide has renal benefits in T2D.

title: Semaglutide 2.4 mg once a week in adults with overweight or obesity
Oral semaglutide versus subcutaneous liraglutide and placebo in T2D (PIONEER 4)
